problems.title: pyramid pattern

columns.Title: pyramid pattern
columns.Description: Build a centered pyramid of * characters using loops.

problems.description: Build a centered pyramid of * characters using loops.

content.statement: Given an integer n, build a centered pyramid pattern using * where row i contains (2*i - 1) stars and leading spaces so that the pyramid is centered.

examples.explanation: Each row grows by 2 stars and decreases one leading space.
Single row is the smallest valid pyramid.

problems.title: Deep Omit

columns.Title: Deep Omit
columns.Description: Remove specified keys from a deeply nested object or array.

problems.description: Remove specified keys from a deeply nested object or array.

content.statement: Given a nested object/array and a list of keys, remove those keys at every depth while preserving the rest of the structure.

examples.explanation: Key c is removed everywhere in objects, including objects inside arrays.
If key does not exist, object remains unchanged.

problems.title: Debounce

columns.Title: Debounce
columns.Description: Implement a `debounce` function that limits how often a given function can be called.

problems.description: Implement a `debounce` function that limits how often a given function can be called.

content.statement: Implement debounce(fn, wait) so fn runs only after there has been no new call for wait milliseconds. Preserve this and latest arguments.

examples.output: Only one execution at ~190ms with last call arguments
examples.explanation: Timer resets on every new call.
Useful when component unmounts or route changes.

problems.title: Word Search

columns.Title: Word Search
columns.Description: Your task is to find all the words from the list that can be constructed from letters of sequentially adjacent cells in the board.

problems.description: Your task is to find all the words from the list that can be constructed from letters of sequentially adjacent cells in the board.

content.statement: Given a 2D board and a list of words, return all words that can be formed by sequentially adjacent cells (up/down/left/right) without reusing a cell in the same word path.

examples.explanation: Only oath and eat can be traced by valid adjacent paths.
Cell reuse in a single path is not allowed.

complexity.time: O(M*N*4^L) worst case, usually lower with Trie pruning
complexity.space: O(total chars in words + L recursion)

problems.title: Combination Sum

columns.Title: Combination Sum
columns.Description: Write a function that returns all unique combinations of candidates where the chosen numbers sum to the target.

problems.description: Write a function that returns all unique combinations of candidates where the chosen numbers sum to the target.

content.statement: Given distinct candidate numbers and a target, return all unique combinations where chosen numbers sum to target. You can use a candidate multiple times.

examples.explanation: Both combinations sum to 7.
No valid combination exists.

problems.title: Map Async Limit

columns.Title: Map Async Limit
columns.Description: Run an asynchronous function over an array with limited concurrency.

problems.description: Run an asynchronous function over an array with limited concurrency.

content.statement: Run an async mapper over an array while allowing at most limit operations in parallel, and return results in input order.

examples.input: items = [1,2,3,4], limit = 2, mapper = async x => x * 2
examples.explanation: Only 2 mapper calls run concurrently at any time.
Empty input returns immediately.

problems.title: Search Rotated Array

columns.Title: Search Rotated Array
columns.Description: Search for a target in a rotated sorted array

problems.description: Search for a target in a rotated sorted array

content.statement: Given a rotated sorted array with distinct elements, return the index of target using O(log n) time, or -1 if not present.

examples.explanation: 0 is located at index 4.
3 does not exist in the array.

problems.title: Sum of Consecutive Integers

columns.Title: Sum of Consecutive Integers
columns.Description: Determine if a given number can be expressed as the sum of two or more consecutive positive integers.

problems.description: Determine if a given number can be expressed as the sum of two or more consecutive positive integers.

content.statement: Return true if n can be represented as the sum of two or more consecutive positive integers, otherwise false.

examples.explanation: 8 cannot be written as sum of >=2 consecutive positive integers.

problems.title: PromiseAll With Concurrency Limit

columns.Title: PromiseAll With Concurrency Limit
columns.Description: Implement a function that works like `Promise.all()` but with a concurrency limit.

problems.description: Implement a function that works like `Promise.all()` but with a concurrency limit.

content.statement: Implement Promise.all behavior for an array of async task functions, but run at most limit tasks in parallel and preserve result order.

examples.explanation: Runs sequentially due to limit=1 but keeps output ordering.

examples.output: Promise rejects immediately with that error
examples.explanation: Matches Promise.all fail-fast behavior.

problems.title: JSON.stringify

columns.Title: JSON.stringify
columns.Description: Implement a custom version of `JSON.stringify`.

problems.description: Implement a custom version of `JSON.stringify`.

content.statement: Implement a custom JSON stringify function for primitives, arrays, and objects with cycle detection and behavior close to native JSON.stringify.

examples.explanation: Objects and arrays are serialized recursively.
Circular structures cannot be serialized to standard JSON.

problems.title: List Format

columns.Title: List Format
columns.Description: Write a function that takes an array of strings and returns a human-readable list

problems.description: Write a function that takes an array of strings and returns a human-readable list

content.statement: Convert an array of strings into a human-readable list, for example: ["A","B","C"] -> "A, B, and C".

examples.explanation: Three or more items are comma-separated with conjunction before the last item.
Two items are joined directly with conjunction.

problems.title: Reverse Words in a String

columns.Title: Reverse Words in a String
columns.Description: Given an input string s, reverse the order of the words.

problems.description: Given an input string s, reverse the order of the words.

content.statement: Given a string, reverse the order of words and return a string with single spaces between words and no leading/trailing spaces.

examples.explanation: Extra spaces are normalized.
Word order is reversed while each word remains unchanged.

problems.title: Throttle

columns.Title: Throttle
columns.Description: Implement a Throttle Function

problems.description: Implement a Throttle Function

content.statement: Implement throttle(fn, wait) so fn executes at most once per wait milliseconds, with configurable leading/trailing behavior.

examples.explanation: Intermediate calls are skipped/coalesced depending on options.

examples.output: First call is delayed; latest args fire at end of window
examples.explanation: Useful for scroll/resize updates.

problems.title: Flatten Nested Objects

columns.Title: Flatten Nested Objects
columns.Description: Write a function that flattens a nested JavaScript object into a single-level object, where keys represent the path to each value using dot notation.

problems.description: Write a function that flattens a nested JavaScript object into a single-level object, where keys represent the path to each value using dot notation.

content.statement: Flatten a nested object into a single-level object where keys represent paths using dot notation (and array indices when needed).

examples.explanation: Nested keys are joined by dots.
Array indices are preserved in bracket format.

problems.title: Priority Queue

columns.Title: Priority Queue
columns.Description: Implement a Priority Queue data structure in JavaScript from scratch.

problems.description: Implement a Priority Queue data structure in JavaScript from scratch.

content.statement: Implement a priority queue using a binary heap. Support insertion, peek, pop/remove highest-priority item, and size queries.

examples.explanation: Smallest element is extracted first in a min-heap.
Size tracks number of elements in heap.

problems.title: Find the Single Element in a Sorted Array

columns.Title: Find the Single Element in a Sorted Array
columns.Description: Your task is to find and return the element that appears only once.

problems.description: Your task is to find and return the element that appears only once.

content.statement: In a sorted array where every element appears exactly twice except one element that appears once, find that single element in O(log n).

examples.explanation: 2 is the only value without a pair.
Binary search uses pair-index alignment.

problems.title: Flatten Deep Object

columns.Title: Flatten Deep Object
columns.Description: Flatten a nested object using dot notation (e.g., `{a: {b: 1}}` → `{'a.b': 1}`).

problems.description: Flatten a nested object using dot notation (e.g., `{a: {b: 1}}` → `{'a.b': 1}`).

content.statement: Flatten a nested object into dot-separated key paths. For arrays, index positions are included as part of the key path.

examples.explanation: All leaf nodes become flat entries.
Empty object is preserved as a leaf value.

problems.title: Topological Sort (DFS)

columns.Title: Topological Sort (DFS)
columns.Description: Find a linear ordering of vertices in a directed acyclic graph (DAG) such that for every directed edge (u, v), vertex u comes before vertex v.

problems.description: Find a linear ordering of vertices in a directed acyclic graph (DAG) such that for every directed edge (u, v), vertex u comes before vertex v.

content.statement: Given a DAG with vertices labeled 0..V-1 and directed edges [u,v], return a topological ordering using DFS. Return [] when a cycle exists.

examples.explanation: Every edge direction is respected in the ordering.
Cycle means no topological ordering exists.

problems.title: Implement Promise Race

columns.Title: Implement Promise Race
columns.Description: Polyfill `Promise.race()` that resolves or rejects as soon as the first promise in the iterable settles.

problems.description: Polyfill `Promise.race()` that resolves or rejects as soon as the first promise in the iterable settles.

content.statement: Polyfill Promise.race(iterable): return a promise that settles as soon as the first input promise/value settles.

examples.explanation: Fastest settled promise wins.
First settled can also be a rejection.

problems.title: Lazy Evaluation

columns.Title: Lazy Evaluation
columns.Description: Create a lazy function evaluator that allows chaining of functions

problems.description: Create a lazy function evaluator that allows chaining of functions

content.statement: Create a lazy evaluator that supports function chaining and executes only when value() (or evaluate()) is called.

examples.explanation: Execution happens at value(), not during chaining.
Custom transformations are queued and applied in order.

complexity.time: O(k) for k chained operations at evaluation

problems.title: Deep Clone Object

columns.Title: Deep Clone Object
columns.Description: Create a function that performs a deep clone of a given JavaScript object.

problems.description: Create a function that performs a deep clone of a given JavaScript object.

content.statement: Implement deep clone for nested JavaScript values (objects, arrays, Date, RegExp, Map, Set) while handling circular references.

examples.output: cloned object with independent nested references
examples.explanation: Changing clone.b.c should not mutate original.b.c.
WeakMap avoids infinite recursion for cycles.

complexity.space: O(N) including cloned structure and seen map

problems.title: Detect Cycle in an Undirected Connected Graph (DFS)

columns.Title: Detect Cycle in an Undirected Connected Graph (DFS)
columns.Description: Determine whether the component that includes node 0 in an undirected graph contains a cycle using a straightforward depth-first search with parent tracking

problems.description: Determine whether the component that includes node 0 in an undirected graph contains a cycle using a straightforward depth-first search with parent tracking

content.statement: Given an undirected graph and node count n, detect whether the connected component containing node 0 has a cycle using DFS and parent tracking.

examples.explanation: Component containing 0 has cycle 0-1-2-0.
Component containing 0 is a simple chain without back-edge.

complexity.time: O(V + E) for reachable component from 0

problems.title: Rate Limiter

columns.Title: Rate Limiter
columns.Description: Implement a sliding window rate limiter that processes API requests and determines which ones should be allowed.

problems.description: Implement a sliding window rate limiter that processes API requests and determines which ones should be allowed.

content.statement: Implement a sliding-window rate limiter that allows at most limit requests in the last windowMs milliseconds per key (for example user/IP).

examples.input: limit=3, windowMs=1000, requests at t=[0,100,200,300] for same key
examples.explanation: 4th request in same window is blocked.

examples.input: After enough time passes beyond window
examples.explanation: Expired timestamps are removed from window.

problems.title: Detect data type in JS

columns.Title: Detect data type in JS
columns.Description: Create a function `detectType()` that returns the data type of the given input.

problems.description: Create a function `detectType()` that returns the data type of the given input.

content.statement: Create detectType(value) that returns a precise type string for common JavaScript values (null, array, date, map, set, etc.).

examples.explanation: Array must be identified separately from object.
NaN is a special numeric value.

problems.title: Sum of Subarray Minimum

columns.Title: Sum of Subarray Minimum
columns.Description: Given an array of integers arr, find the sum of min(b)

problems.description: Given an array of integers arr, find the sum of min(b)

content.statement: Given an array arr, compute the sum of the minimum value of every contiguous subarray. Return result modulo 1e9+7.

examples.explanation: Subarray minimum sum is 17.
Monotonic stack computes each element contribution efficiently.

problems.title: Topological Sort (BFS)

columns.Title: Topological Sort (BFS)
columns.Description: Find a topological ordering of vertices in a directed acyclic graph using in-degree counting and BFS.
columns.Companies: Atlassian, Adobe, Amazon, Google, Linkedin, Microsoft, Phonepe

problems.description: Find a topological ordering of vertices in a directed acyclic graph using in-degree counting and BFS.

content.statement: Given a directed graph, return a topological ordering using Kahn’s algorithm (BFS with in-degree). Return [] if cycle exists.

examples.explanation: Any order respecting all directed edges is valid.
Cycle prevents full topological ordering.

problems.title: Concurrency Limited Task Scheduler

columns.Title: Concurrency Limited Task Scheduler
columns.Description: You are given an array of asynchronous functions called tasks, where each function returns a Promise resolving to a value.

problems.description: You are given an array of asynchronous functions called tasks, where each function returns a Promise resolving to a value.

content.statement: Given an array of async task functions and a concurrency limit, execute all tasks while running at most limit tasks simultaneously and return ordered results.

examples.explanation: Tasks complete in any order internally but output aligns to input index.
Error is propagated to caller.

problems.title: Spiral Matrix Pathfinder

columns.Title: Spiral Matrix Pathfinder
columns.Description: Traverse a 2D matrix in a spiral pattern, collecting all non-blocked values while avoiding obstacles marked as -1.
columns.Companies: Google, Meta, Microsoft, Amazon, Uber, Adobe

problems.description: Traverse a 2D matrix in a spiral pattern, collecting all non-blocked values while avoiding obstacles marked as -1.

content.statement: Traverse a matrix in spiral order and collect values while skipping blocked cells marked as -1.

examples.explanation: Center blocked cell is skipped during traversal.
Only cell is blocked.

problems.title: Event Emitter

columns.Title: Event Emitter
columns.Description: Design and implement an `EventEmitter` class that mimics Node.js-style event handling.

problems.description: Design and implement an `EventEmitter` class that mimics Node.js-style event handling.

content.statement: Design an EventEmitter class supporting on, off, once, and emit similar to Node.js event handling.

examples.explanation: Registered listeners receive emitted arguments.
once auto-unsubscribes after first call.

complexity.time: emit O(k) where k=listeners for event

problems.title: Search in a 2D Sorted Matrix

columns.Title: Search in a 2D Sorted Matrix
columns.Description: You are given a 2D matrix of integers where each row is sorted in ascending order from left to right,

problems.description: You are given a 2D matrix of integers where each row is sorted in ascending order from left to right,

content.statement: Given a matrix where each row is sorted and rows follow global sorted order (first element of row i is greater than last element of row i-1), determine if target exists.

examples.explanation: Binary search over virtual flattened array finds target.
6 is not present in matrix.

problems.title: Merge Array

columns.Title: Merge Array
columns.Description: Merge the array of object data by `id`

problems.description: Merge the array of object data by `id`

content.statement: Merge multiple arrays of objects by id. If same id appears multiple times, merge properties with later arrays overriding earlier ones.

examples.explanation: id=1 objects are merged into one.
Only records with valid id participate in merge.

problems.title: Memoization

columns.Title: Memoization
columns.Description: Implement a memoization function that takes a function as an argument and returns a memoized version of that function.

problems.description: Implement a memoization function that takes a function as an argument and returns a memoized version of that function.

content.statement: Implement a memoize utility that caches function results based on arguments so repeated calls avoid recomputation.

examples.output: Second call returns instantly from cache
examples.explanation: Stored result is reused for same arguments.

examples.output: Can be cached separately unless custom resolver normalizes key
examples.explanation: Cache key strategy is configurable.

complexity.time: O(1) average cache lookup + function cost on miss

problems.title: Shortest Distance from Source (BFS)

columns.Title: Shortest Distance from Source (BFS)
columns.Description: Find the shortest distance from a source node to all other nodes in an unweighted graph using Breadth First Search (BFS).

problems.description: Find the shortest distance from a source node to all other nodes in an unweighted graph using Breadth First Search (BFS).

content.statement: Given an unweighted graph with n nodes and edges, compute shortest distance from source to all nodes using BFS. Unreachable nodes get -1.

examples.explanation: BFS discovers nodes layer-by-layer.
Nodes 2 and 3 are disconnected from source.

problems.title: Longest Substring No Repeats

columns.Title: Longest Substring No Repeats
columns.Description: Find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters (e.g., `"abcabcbb"` → `3`)

problems.description: Find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters (e.g., `"abcabcbb"` → `3`)

content.statement: Return the length of the longest substring without repeating characters using a sliding-window approach.

examples.explanation: Longest substrings are "abc", length 3.
Only one unique char at a time.

problems.title: Object.assign()

columns.Title: Object.assign()
columns.Description: Recreate the functionality of `Object.assign()` in JavaScript.

problems.description: Recreate the functionality of `Object.assign()` in JavaScript.

content.statement: Recreate Object.assign(target, ...sources): copy enumerable own properties (including symbols) from sources to target and return target.

examples.explanation: Later sources override earlier values for same key.
Target cannot be null or undefined.

problems.title: Oranges Rotting

columns.Title: Oranges Rotting
columns.Description: Write a function that determines the number of minutes for all fresh oranges to become rotten.

problems.description: Write a function that determines the number of minutes for all fresh oranges to become rotten.

content.statement: In a grid, 0=empty, 1=fresh orange, 2=rotten orange. Every minute, fresh oranges adjacent to rotten ones become rotten. Return minutes needed to rot all fresh oranges, or -1 if impossible.

examples.explanation: BFS from all initially rotten oranges tracks minute levels.
Some fresh oranges remain isolated forever.

problems.title: Compress String with Limited Repetition

columns.Title: Compress String with Limited Repetition
columns.Description: Compress the string by replacing consecutive repeated characters with the character followed by the count of its repetitions.

problems.description: Compress the string by replacing consecutive repeated characters with the character followed by the count of its repetitions.

content.statement: Compress a string using run-length encoding by replacing repeated consecutive characters with char + count. Optionally split counts by maxRun.

examples.explanation: Single occurrence keeps only character; repeated ones append count.
Runs are split so each encoded chunk count <= maxRun.
